body_txt: When it comes to attacks on the human face, the nose is one of the biggest targets after the eyes and the mouth : after all, it's the first element of the face that's within reach. Though it doesn't have quite the same visceral response as attacking the eyes or the teeth , it's a very obvious vulnerability with its own special brand of pain and visual flair attached. Hitting someone in the ear , for example, is painful but not quite as visually distinct , thereby not nearly so dramatic ; in fiction, breaking someone's nose is not only noticeable thanks to the spectacular nosebleed, but it's often considered a sign that the attacker has done some serious damage. As anyone who's experienced this in real life knows, a broken nose is debilitating in the moment: the pain is immediate, the impact is disorienting, breathing may be impeded, blood flows in excess, and the nose may end up permanently altered if not properly treated. With this in mind, it's no surprise that bloody or broken noses are a recognizable facial injury in fistfights, a visual shorthand for "you've just been hit hard." When it comes to more serious injuries, there's a reason why characters with no noses appear unusual in live-action works: the nose plays a very important role in the human perception of the face, and without it, the entire face looks wrong... and that's not even getting into the horror of having the nose reduced to a gaping hole , for example. As such, going for the nose is one of the most common tactics in facial mutilation as a means of leaving the victim noticeably scarred for life. Long story short, this trope deals with attacks on the nose of any kind. If they cause pain, they're here.  Depending on the severity of the attack, this can result in the character becoming The Noseless . In a written work, the character may subsequently talk in Congestion Speak . Extreme cases may involve Facial Horror or Cold-Blooded Torture of some kind. For funny cases, this trope occurs with a "Got Your Nose" Gag , as someone pretends to steal another person’s nose as a game. But there are dark examples when someone actually pulls off a person’s nose. Compare Agony of the Feet , Ear Ache , Fingore , and Tongue Trauma for other injuries to specific body parts. Compare Psychic Nosebleed , when using psychic powers results someone getting a nosebleed. Contrast Nasal Weapon .
